mn50:1.2: At one time Venerable Mahāmoggallāna was dwelling among the Bhaggas at Susumāragiri, in Bhesakaḷā Grove, in the deer park.
mn50:10.1: But Venerable Sañjīva, whether he had gone to the forest, to the root of a tree, or to an empty hut, would very easily enter the attainment of cessation of perception and feeling.
mn50:10.2: Formerly, Evil One, Venerable Sañjīva was sitting at the root of a certain tree, having entered the attainment of cessation of perception and feeling.
mn50:10.3: Cowherds, shepherds, farmers, and travelers saw Venerable Sañjīva sitting at the root of a certain tree, having entered the attainment of cessation of perception and feeling.
mn50:10.4: Seeing him, this occurred to them:
mn50:10.5: ‘It is amazing, sirs, it is astounding, sirs!
mn50:10.6: This ascetic has died while still seated.
mn50:10.8: Then, Evil One, those cowherds, shepherds, farmers, and travelers gathered grass, wood, and cow dung, piled it up on Venerable Sañjīva’s body, set it on fire, and went away.
mn50:11.1: Then, Evil One, when that night had passed, Venerable Sañjīva emerged from that attainment, shook out his robes, and in the morning dressed, took his bowl and robe, and entered the village for alms.
mn50:11.2: Those cowherds, shepherds, farmers, and travelers saw Venerable Sañjīva going on almsround.
mn50:11.3: Seeing him, this occurred to them:
mn50:11.4: ‘It is amazing, sirs, it is astounding, sirs!
mn50:11.5: This ascetic died while still seated, and now he has come to life again.’
mn50:11.6: It was in this way, Evil One, that the designation ‘Sañjīva’ came to Venerable Sañjīva.
mn50:12.1: Then, Evil One, it occurred to Dūsī Māra:
mn50:12.2: ‘I do not know the coming or going of these *bhikkhus* who are virtuous and of good qualities.
mn50:12.3: What if I were to possess brahmins and householders—
mn50:12.4: “Come, you, abuse, insult, anger, and harass those *bhikkhus* who are virtuous and of good qualities.
mn50:12.5: Perhaps when they are abused, insulted, angered, and harassed by you, there might be a change in mind [*cittassa aññathattaṁ*], so that Dūsī Māra could find an opening.”’
mn50:12.6: Then, Evil One, Dūsī Māra possessed brahmins and householders:
mn50:12.7: ‘Come, you, abuse, insult, anger, and harass those *bhikkhus* who are virtuous and of good qualities.
mn50:12.8: Perhaps when they are abused, insulted, angered, and harassed by you, there might be a change in mind [*cittassa aññathattaṁ*], so that Dūsī Māra could find an opening.’
mn50:13.1: Then, Evil One, those brahmins and householders, possessed by Dūsī Māra, abused, insulted, angered, and harassed those *bhikkhus* who were virtuous and of good qualities:
mn50:13.10: in the same way these shaveling little ascetics, menials, dark fellows, born from the feet of their kinsmen, saying “We are meditators, we are meditators,” with drooping shoulders, faces downcast, Madhura-bred, meditate, keep meditating, inwardly meditate, and over-meditate.’
mn50:13.11: And most of the people who died at that time, Evil One, after the breakup of the body, after death, were reborn in a state of loss, a bad destination, ruin, hell.
mn50:13.2: ‘These shaveling little ascetics, menials, dark fellows, born from the feet of their kinsmen, saying “We are meditators, we are meditators,” with drooping shoulders, faces downcast, Madhura-bred, meditate, keep meditating, inwardly meditate, and over-meditate.
mn50:13.3: Just as an owl perched on a tree branch, searching for a mouse, meditates, keeps meditating, inwardly meditates, and over-meditates;
mn50:13.4: in the same way these shaveling little ascetics, menials, dark fellows, born from the feet of their kinsmen, saying “We are meditators, we are meditators,” with drooping shoulders, faces downcast, Madhura-bred, meditate, keep meditating, inwardly meditate, and over-meditate.
mn50:13.5: Just as a jackal on a riverbank, searching for fish, meditates, keeps meditating, inwardly meditates, and over-meditates;
mn50:13.6: in the same way these shaveling little ascetics, menials, dark fellows, born from the feet of their kinsmen, saying “We are meditators, we are meditators,” with drooping shoulders, faces downcast, Madhura-bred, meditate, keep meditating, inwardly meditate, and over-meditate.
mn50:13.7: Just as a cat by the edge of a drain opening, searching for a mouse, meditates, keeps meditating, inwardly meditates, and over-meditates;
mn50:13.8: in the same way these shaveling little ascetics, menials, dark fellows, born from the feet of their kinsmen, saying “We are meditators, we are meditators,” with drooping shoulders, faces downcast, Madhura-bred, meditate, keep meditating, inwardly meditate, and over-meditate.
mn50:13.9: Just as a donkey with its load cut off, at the edge of a drain opening, meditates, keeps meditating, inwardly meditates, and over-meditates;
mn50:14.1: Then, Evil One, the *Bhagavā* Kakusandha, the *arahant*, the perfectly awakened one, addressed the *bhikkhus*:
mn50:14.2: ‘*Bhikkhus*, brahmins and householders have been possessed by Dūsī Māra—
mn50:14.3: “Come, you, abuse, insult, anger, and harass those *bhikkhus* who are virtuous and of good qualities. Perhaps when they are abused, insulted, angered, and harassed by you, there might be a change in mind [*cittassa aññathattaṁ*], so that Dūsī Māra could find an opening.”
mn50:14.4: Come, *bhikkhus*, dwell pervading one direction with a mind [*cetasā*] imbued with goodwill [*mettā*], likewise the second, likewise the third, likewise the fourth. So above, below, across, everywhere, and in every respect, dwell pervading the whole world with a mind [*cetasā*] imbued with goodwill [*mettā*], abundant, expansive, immeasurable, without enmity, without ill will.
mn50:14.5: With a mind [*cetasā*] imbued with compassion [*karuṇā*] …
mn50:14.6: with a mind [*cetasā*] imbued with gladness [*muditā*] …
mn50:14.7: with a mind [*cetasā*] imbued with equanimity [*upekkhā*], dwell pervading one direction, likewise the second, likewise the third, likewise the fourth. So above, below, across, everywhere, and in every respect, dwell pervading the whole world with a mind [*cetasā*] imbued with equanimity [*upekkhā*], abundant, expansive, immeasurable, without enmity, without ill will.’
mn50:15.1: Then, Evil One, those *bhikkhus*, being thus advised and instructed by the *Bhagavā* Kakusandha, the *arahant*, the perfectly awakened one, whether they had gone to the forest, to the root of a tree, or to an empty hut, dwelled pervading one direction with a mind [*cetasā*] imbued with goodwill [*mettā*], likewise the second, likewise the third, likewise the fourth. So above, below, across, everywhere, and in every respect, they dwelled pervading the whole world with a mind [*cetasā*] imbued with goodwill [*mettā*], abundant, expansive, immeasurable, without enmity, without ill will.
mn50:15.2: With a mind [*cetasā*] imbued with compassion [*karuṇā*] …
mn50:15.3: with a mind [*cetasā*] imbued with gladness [*muditā*] …
mn50:15.4: with a mind [*cetasā*] imbued with equanimity [*upekkhā*], they dwelled pervading one direction, likewise the second, likewise the third, likewise the fourth. So above, below, across, everywhere, and in every respect, they dwelled pervading the whole world with a mind [*cetasā*] imbued with equanimity [*upekkhā*], abundant, expansive, immeasurable, without enmity, without ill will.
mn50:16.1: Then, Evil One, it occurred to Dūsī Māra:
mn50:16.2: ‘Even while doing this, I still do not know the coming or going of these *bhikkhus* who are virtuous and of good qualities. What if I were to possess brahmins and householders:
mn50:16.3: “Come, you, honor, respect, esteem, and venerate those *bhikkhus* who are virtuous and of good qualities.
mn50:16.4: Perhaps when they are honored, respected, esteemed, and venerated by you, there might be a change in mind [*cittassa aññathattaṁ*], so that Dūsī Māra could find an opening.”’
mn50:17.1: Then, Evil One, Dūsī Māra possessed brahmins and householders:
mn50:17.2: ‘Come, you, honor, respect, esteem, and venerate those *bhikkhus* who are virtuous and of good qualities.
mn50:17.3: Perhaps when they are honored, respected, esteemed, and venerated by you, there might be a change in mind [*cittassa aññathattaṁ*], so that Dūsī Māra could find an opening.’
mn50:17.4: Then, Evil One, those brahmins and householders, possessed by Dūsī Māra, honored, respected, esteemed, and venerated those *bhikkhus* who were virtuous and of good qualities.
mn50:17.5: And most of the people who died at that time, Evil One, after the breakup of the body, after death, were reborn in a good destination, in a heavenly world.
mn50:18.1: Then, Evil One, the *Bhagavā* Kakusandha, the *arahant*, the perfectly awakened one, addressed the *bhikkhus*:
mn50:18.2: ‘*Bhikkhus*, brahmins and householders have been possessed by Dūsī Māra:
mn50:18.3: “Come, you, honor, respect, esteem, and venerate those *bhikkhus* who are virtuous and of good qualities.
mn50:18.4: Perhaps when they are honored, respected, esteemed, and venerated by you, there might be a change in mind [*cittassa aññathattaṁ*], so that Dūsī Māra could find an opening.”
mn50:18.5: Come, *bhikkhus*, dwell contemplating the unattractive in the body, with *saññā* of repulsiveness in food [*paṭikūlasaññino*], with *saññā* of non-delight in the whole world [*anabhiratisaññino*], contemplating all *saṅkhāras* as impermanent [*aniccānupassino*].’
mn50:19.1: Then, Evil One, those *bhikkhus*, being thus advised and instructed by the *Bhagavā* Kakusandha, the *arahant*, the perfectly awakened one, whether they had gone to the forest, to the root of a tree, or to an empty hut, dwelled contemplating the unattractive in the body, with *saññā* of repulsiveness in food [*paṭikūlasaññino*], with *saññā* of non-delight in the whole world [*anabhiratisaññino*], contemplating all *saṅkhāras* as impermanent [*aniccānupassino*].

mn50:2.5: I suppose it is filled with beans.”
mn50:2.6: Then Venerable Mahāmoggallāna came down from his walking path, entered his dwelling, and sat down on the seat laid out.
mn50:20.1: Then, Evil One, in the morning the *Bhagavā* Kakusandha, the *arahant*, the perfectly awakened one, dressed, took his bowl and robe, and entered the village for alms with Venerable Vidhura following behind him.
mn50:21.1: Then, Evil One, Dūsī Māra possessed a certain boy, who picked up a stone and struck Venerable Vidhura on the head, splitting his head open.
mn50:21.2: Then, Evil One, Venerable Vidhura, with his head split open and blood flowing, followed right behind the *Bhagavā* Kakusandha, the *arahant*, the perfectly awakened one.
mn50:21.3: Then, Evil One, the *Bhagavā* Kakusandha, the *arahant*, the perfectly awakened one, looked back as an elephant does:
mn50:21.4: ‘This Dūsī Māra has not known his measure.’
mn50:21.5: And, Evil One, at that very glance Dūsī Māra fell from that place and was reborn in the Great Hell.
mn50:22.1: Now, Evil One, that Great Hell has three names—
mn50:22.2: ‘The Dimension of Six Contacts’ [*chaphassāyataniko*], ‘Struck by Stakes’, and ‘To Be Felt Individually’.

mn50:22.6: “I have been roasting in hell for a thousand years.”’
mn50:23.1: So, Evil One, I roasted in that Great Hell for many years, for many hundreds of years, for many thousands of years.
mn50:23.2: For ten thousand years I roasted in an outlying region of that same Great Hell, experiencing a feeling called ‘Emergence’.
mn50:23.3: There, Evil One, I had a body like that of a human being.
mn50:23.4: And I had a head like that of a fish.

mn50:8.1: Formerly, Evil One, I was a Māra named Dūsī, and I had a sister named Kālī.
mn50:9.1: Now at that time, Evil One, the *Bhagavā* Kakusandha, an *arahant*, a perfectly awakened one, had arisen in the world.
mn50:9.2: And for the *Bhagavā* Kakusandha, the *arahant*, the perfectly awakened one, there was a pair of disciples named Vidhura and Sañjīva, the foremost and excellent pair.
mn50:9.3: As many disciples as there were of the *Bhagavā* Kakusandha, the *arahant*, the perfectly awakened one—
mn50:9.4: among them none was equal or comparable to Venerable Vidhura in teaching *dhamma*.
mn50:9.5: It was in this way, Evil One, that the designation ‘Vidhura’ came to Venerable Vidhura.
